items.title: Jewish ANU student called 'baby killer' on campus, royal commission hears

children.content: The Royal Commission into Anti Semitism and Social Cohesion has heard universities need to improve responses when Jewish staff and students feel unsafe.

image.altText: A woman with long dark hair in a dark blazer and white shirt stands behind a microphone looking off camera.

items.title: ACT signs deal for CSIRO site, plans to build 3,000 homes

children.content: After being flagged for development over a decade ago, the ACT government has reached an agreement for the sale of land at a former CSIRO agriculture research site to construction of thousands new homes.

image.altText: A drone shot of green pastures

items.title: As the mercury drops in Canberra, cyclists are still braving the chill

children.content: Every year, winter sees many Canberrans hanging up their helmets, despite the city being considered Australia's "cycling capital." This year though, cyclist numbers have remained relatively steady, even as the mercury drops.

gallery.altText: A man rides along on a cargo-bike.

thumbnail.altText: A man rides along on a cargo-bike.

items.title: 'He gave us 10 extra years': Australian-first immune centre opens in Canberra

children.content: The Centre for Personalised Medicine at Canberra Hospital uses cutting-edge technology to identify the causes of complex immune conditions to tailor treatments for patients.

image.altText: An elderly man and his adult daughter smiling outside.

items.title: First Indigenous Military Cross recipient discovered by War Memorial

children.content: Captain Sidney Leonard Morse's achievements would have remained hidden were it not for a chance discovery by a volunteer historian collating family trees at the Australian War Memorial.

image.altText: A Military Cross medal in front of a line of other military medals.

items.title: Aboriginal artist recreating 'oldest living weaving practice' with glass

children.content: Lower Southern Arrernte woman Jenni Kemarre Martiniello creates glass artworks by combining a centuries-old Italian glassmaking technique with millennia-old Indigenous weaving practices.

gallery.altText: A person's hands working on a woven-patterned glass sculpture with a blowtorch.
A woman with short grey hair stands in a studio surrounded by glass artworks with woven patterns of colour.
A woman with short grey hair stands in a studio looking at the weaving of an handmade bag.

thumbnail.altText: A person's hands working on a woven-patterned glass sculpture with a blowtorch.
A woman with short grey hair stands in a studio surrounded by glass artworks with woven patterns of colour.
A woman with short grey hair stands in a studio looking at the weaving of an handmade bag.

tags.title: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Art

items.title: In Barrow Creek a $5 note hangs on the pub's wall, still waiting for Peter Falconio

children.content: Twenty-five years ago, the murder of British backpacker Peter Falconio and the abduction and escape of his girlfriend Joanne Lees in the remote Central Australian outback captured global attention. Today, it remains one of the nation's most infamous cases.

gallery.altText: A old photo of couple sitting in the front seats of a Kombi van, they are turned around to look at the camera.
An Australian five dollar note stuck to a wall with other notes and papers reading PETER + PAUL FALCONIO
A man in a pale shirt is held by a serious-looking police officer.

thumbnail.altText: A old photo of couple sitting in the front seats of a Kombi van, they are turned around to look at the camera.
An Australian five dollar note stuck to a wall with other notes and papers reading PETER + PAUL FALCONIO
A man in a pale shirt is held by a serious-looking police officer.

items.title: Man left 'without an identity' after 'mistake' he made 45 years ago

children.content: Kwong Wan Lo stowed away on a ship bound for Australia 45 years ago. With no legal right to stay, he built a life in Sydney anyway.

image.altText: A portrait of Kwong Wan Lo wearing a black jacket.

items.title: ICE agents shoot and kill driver who witnesses say 'tried to stop'

children.content: A federal immigration officer has fatally shot a motorist in Maine, the second time in a week that US Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents have used deadly force.

image.altText: A woman at a park holding a sign saying 'ICE not welcome here'

items.title: 'Jurassic family': Spielberg leads outpouring of tributes for actor Sam Neill

children.content: The New Zealand actor died suddenly at the age of 78, with Hollywood directors and actors and prominent political figures all paying tribute to a great of global cinema.

gallery.altText: Sam Neill in character as Dr Alan Grant in Jurassic Park, standing in the rain wearing an akubra.
Black and white image smiling Jeff Goldblum, Laura Dern and Sam Neill

items.title: Police arrest alleged organiser behind Melbourne's hospitality arson attacks

children.content: Investigators say the 20-year-old man tasked offenders with home invasions, kidnappings and arsons targeting the city's nightlife venues.

image.altText: Police tape at a crime scene

items.title: Australian lamb new favourite in US as protein 'wave' creates demand

children.content: Australians may love their lamb, but it has always been a harder sell for American consumers. There are signs that is changing.

image.altText: Picture of lamb with the American flag

items.title: Shark threat closes popular beaches, snorkelling spots along Ningaloo coast

children.content: Authorities save one animal but another succumbs to its injuries, leading sharks to converge on a popular snorkelling spot.

gallery.altText: Sharks swarm the pale carcass of a humpback whale.

thumbnail.altText: Sharks swarm the pale carcass of a humpback whale.

items.title: Self-managed super funds could have to pay compensation

children.content: The assistant treasurer is looking to revamp funding for the Compensation Scheme of Last Resort, saying that large super funds, as well as people with self-managed super funds (SMSFs), could be asked to chip in.

image.altText: A woman who lost her super sits at home with a sad face

items.title: Property developers are benefiting from Australia's housing crisis

children.content: State and federal governments are offering incentives for property developers in exchange for more affordable housing. But a Four Corners investigation has uncovered major flaws in the strategy.

image.altText: a man in hi-vis observing an apartment

items.title: Negotiating your bills could save you thousands. Here's how to start

children.content: Negotiating to reduce your spending and increase your income are tangible ways you can improve your financial situation right now.

gallery.altText: A concerned woman sits at table with a notebook. The background is illustrated with money symbols.

thumbnail.altText: A concerned woman sits at table with a notebook. The background is illustrated with money symbols.

avatar.alt: Lacey Filipich stands against a wall, smiling, wearing a white top and denim jacket.

items.title: Elon Musk's criticisms of The Odyssey ignore an important fact

children.content: The tech trillionaire has spent a good chunk of 2026 yelling on X about how Christopher Nolan's adaptation of Homer's Odyssey "desecrates" the ancient poet.

image.altText: Soliders in a forest

items.title: ABC Places quiz: Where in the world are we?

children.content: Your challenge in the ABC Places quiz is to work out the location of 10 photos from around the world. Be warned: we might try to trick you.

image.altText: A pair of dogs wear Norway's flag, a Viking helmet and a blond ponytail hairpiece suggestive of striker Erling Haaland's hair

items.title: Signs of sugar detected near centre of the Milky Way

children.content: Astronomers have detected signs of a type of sugar found in raspberries in gas clouds near the centre of our galaxy the Milky Way.

gallery.altText: Red coloured image of gas clouds and millions of stars at centre of Milky Way.
A large telescope dish pointing to the sky during the day.

thumbnail.altText: Red coloured image of gas clouds and millions of stars at centre of Milky Way.

items.title: 'Coordinator' of Sydney grandfather's mistaken kidnapping murder charged

children.content: A man accused of coordinating the alleged murder and kidnapping of Sydney grandfather Chris Baghsarian has been charged.

image.altText: A man with long dark hair sits on the ground. A police officer in riot squad uniform stands over him.

items.title: Businessman shuts down council by laying charges against councillors

children.content: Three more councillors at Hepburn Shire Council have been served with criminal charges by a private citizen, leaving only two councillors in office.

image.altText: A historic building in a main street.

items.title: Nurse charged with sexually assaulting patient in aged care facility

children.content: An assistant nurse has been charged with sexually assaulting an elderly, cognitively impaired patient at an aged care facility in northern NSW.

image.altText: Generic image of NSW Police officer.

items.title: 'Kill him': Muslim man attacked on way to mosque

children.content: A Muslim man has escaped a near-miss knife attack on his way to evening prayers after police say he was set upon by two men metres away from his local mosque.

image.altText: A photo of Samir in the hospital. His lip and eyes are bleeding and bruised.

items.title: 'Gatekeeping septuagenarians': 'Inappropriate' TikTok video deleted

children.content: The National Trust of SA says one of its volunteers apologised after posting a social media clip labelling a group of disaffected members "gatekeeping septuagenarians" who were running a "stuffy museum".

image.altText: "Sex is great... but have you ever experienced the euphoria of ripping down every single no photo sign in a stuffy museum?"

items.title: 'I have everything to ruin your life': Sextortion threats on the rise

children.content: Major online platforms are accused of failing to deploy readily available technology to detect well-known blackmail tactics.

image.altText: Girl at computer with emojis and speech bubble reading 'Are you alone?'

items.title: Violence against women under national spotlight after four deaths in four days

children.content: Experts are calling for more research and better funding for domestic violence prevention after four women and girls were killed in separate incidents last week.

image.altText: A composite showing photos of three women, and a fourth picture that shows the shadow outline of a girl

items.title: 'Frightening': Organised crime dominates the illicit tobacco market and it's now online

children.content: The process was simple and the service was speedy but the ease with which 7.30 acquired illicit tobacco products was concerning.

items.title: Richard Scolyer encouraged Australians to enrol in research. Here's how

children.content: In his open letter to all Australians, the late Richard Scolyer wrote of the pride he felt taking part in clinical trials. But what exactly is it like to participate?

image.altText: A man sits at a desk and has an eye test by a female health worker.

items.title: Deadly flashover the 'point of no return' for trapped pub patrons: Expert

children.content: A video analysis of an explosive pub fire in Thailand, which killed 27 people, appears to show a flashover "point of no return" that would have made evacuation nearly impossible, an expert says.

image.altText: Flames erupt from pub entrance at nighttime as patrons flee onto street.

items.title: Mixed martial arts group attempts to gift $82k cognac to Brisbane Olympic committee

children.content: The gifts, revealed exclusively by the ABC today, are part of a wider series of examples of attempted influence-peddling from businesses and sporting bodies before the Games kick off in 2032.

image.altText: a bottle of cognac next to two glasses on top of a barrell

items.title: Jewish professor says 'intimidating' protest changed his life on campus

children.content: Melbourne University professor Steven Prawer hopes the Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion will lead to campus change, following a 2024 demonstration that saw protesters invade his office.

image.altText: Man in blue shirt stands in front of office window and indoor plant

items.title: Dutch football 'deeply saddened' as axed World Cup referee dies at 38

children.content: Dutch football mourns the sudden death of "highly valued referee" Rob Dieperink just months after FIFA removed him from the panel of World Cup officials.

image.altText: Rob Dieperink whistles and holds a yellow card while pointing

items.title: De Minaur ends 17-year Australian drought with career-high ranking

children.content: Alex de Minaur's arrow keeps pointing upwards despite his wait for a grand slam title being lengthened by a disappointing Wimbledon exit.

image.altText: A man holds his racquet up to a tennis crowd.

items.title: Caution over radical social media bushwalk plans

children.content: Don't plan a bushwalk based on what an Instagram influencer may have supposedly achieved, a veteran rescuer says, ahead of Tasmania's winter season approaching.

items.title: Ukraine and allies join to protect Europe from ballistic missiles

children.content: Ukraine and nine other countries will form a coalition to protect Europe from ballistic missiles, utilising Kyiv's experience in fighting Russia's full-scale invasion for over four years.

image.altText: A ballistic missile crashing towards the Kyiv skyline.
